meta.name: Chili-Lime Chicken
meta.subtitle: Bright citrus marinade + savory garlic + controlled heat (grill, sear, or roast)

summary: Juicy chicken with a chili-lime-garlic marinade that caramelizes beautifully on the grill or in a skillet. Easy to scale for meal prep.

yield.notes: Serves 4 as a main with sides; 6 in bowls/tacos with extra toppings.

optional.label: Sheet pan (oven method)
optional.notes: Use if roasting instead of grilling/searing.

ingredients.label: Chicken thighs (boneless/skinless preferred)
notes: Or use 6–8 bone-in thighs; increase cook time and verify internal temp.
Chicken breast works too; reduce heat slightly and do not overcook.

ingredients.label: Lime zest
ingredients.notes: If you don’t have a zester, peel thin strips (avoid white pith) and mince finely.

ingredients.label: Chili powder
ingredients.notes: Use mild chili powder for less heat; ancho is great for depth.

ingredients.label: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Optional but recommended for grill-like smokiness.

ingredients.label: Honey (optional)
ingredients.notes: Helps caramelization and rounds sharp citrus/heat.

ingredients.label: Lime wedges (optional)
ingredients.notes: Serve extra lime on the side for brightness.

steps.title: Make the chili-lime marinade
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together lime juice, lime zest, olive oil, minced garlic, chili powder, smoked paprika (if using), cumin, oregano, salt, pepper, and honey (if using).
Taste the marinade: it should be tangy, savory, and slightly spicy. Adjust salt or honey to balance if needed.

steps.title: Marinate the chicken
instructions: Pat chicken dry. Add it to the marinade and toss until evenly coated.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 20 minutes (best: 30–60 minutes).
If short on time, you can cook immediately, but flavor improves with a brief rest.

steps.title: Choose your cook method (grill, skillet, or oven)
instructions: Grill (best flavor): Preheat to medium-high. Oil grates lightly.
Skillet: Heat a skillet over medium-high until hot; add a small splash of oil if needed.
Oven: Preheat to 425°F / 218°C and line a sheet pan.

steps.title: Cook the chicken
instructions: Grill: Cook 4–6 minutes per side depending on thickness until browned and the center reaches 165°F / 74°C.
Skillet: Sear 4–6 minutes per side until well-browned and cooked through. Reduce heat if garlic threatens to burn.
Oven: Spread chicken on sheet pan and roast 16–22 minutes (depending on thickness), flipping once if desired.
checks: Juices run clear; no pink at thickest point
Exterior has browned but garlic is not burnt

steps.title: Rest and finish
instructions: Rest chicken 5 minutes before slicing (keeps it juicy).
Finish with cilantro (optional) and serve with lime wedges.

steps.title: Serve (pairing ideas)
instructions: Plates: serve with rice, roasted vegetables, or salad.
Bowls/tacos: slice and serve with slaw, beans, avocado, and extra lime.
Meal prep: portion with rice + veg; add fresh lime/cilantro at reheat time.

refrigerator.notes: Cool quickly; store in shallow containers.

freezer.notes: Freeze raw chicken in marinade for up to 2 months; thaw in fridge before cooking.

methods: Skillet with a splash of water and cover briefly
reheat.notes: Avoid overcooking on reheat; add fresh lime after warming.

handling: Marinate in the refrigerator, not on the counter.
Discard used marinade that touched raw chicken unless boiled.

variations.label: Mild version
notes: Use mild chili powder and omit any additional hot chilies.
Increase honey to 1 tbsp if you want a gentler heat impression.

variations.label: Hotter version
notes: Add 1 minced jalapeño or 1/4 tsp cayenne.
Finish with a pinch of chili flakes and extra lime zest.

variations.label: Oven + broil finish
notes: Roast until cooked through, then broil 1–2 minutes for charred edges (watch closely).

leftoversPlan.day2: Slice into bowls with rice/beans and a quick slaw.
leftoversPlan.day3: Dice into a salad or wrap with greens + citrus vinaigrette.
